content_hints.description: The Zhentarim Hideout is a location where the Zhentarim are present.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see the warehouse door hangs ajar, its padlock smashed and dangling. Inside, thin bars of moonlight cut through gaps in the boarded windows, falling across a floor of sawdust and dark, dried stains. The air is stale and tastes of iron.
content_hints.perception_success: Fresh bootprints in the sawdust lead deeper into the building. At least six people passed through here recently.

content_hints.description: The Zhentarim Hideout is a ramshackle two-story warehouse on Castle Lane, situated at the back of an outer yard behind a high fence. The gate on the fence is not locked.
content_hints.read_aloud: Crates are stacked high on either side, creating a narrow corridor of bolted cloth and flour sacks that funnels you toward the far doorway. The air smells of salt fish and damp grain. Ahead, overturned tables have been shoved together into a crude barricade, and behind it you can see movement -- dark shapes shifting, the dull gleam of crossbow bolts catching what little light there is.

enemies.name: Zhentarim Thug
enemies.notes: Leather armor, maces. Will surrender below 5 HP.

content_hints.description: The Zhentarim Hideout is a ramshackle two-story warehouse on Castle Lane, standing at the back of an outer yard behind a high fence with an unlocked gate.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a ramshackle two-story warehouse on Castle Lane, a hideout for the Black Network. The building stands at the back of an outer yard behind a high fence, with an unlocked gate.

enemies.name: Zhentarim Spy
enemies.notes: Shortsword, hand crossbow. Cunning Action (bonus action Dash, Disengage, or Hide). Will attempt to flee if outnumbered.

loot.name: Half-Burned Ledger
loot.description: Partially destroyed. Mentions payments to someone called 'The Zhent' and a delivery at Gralhund Villa.

loot.name: Sewer Map (Waterdeep)
loot.description: Marked with three circled locations: Xanathar Guild outpost, Zhent safehouse, and an unmarked cellar in the Trades Ward.

content_hints.investigation_success: Among the unburned papers: a note reading 'Bring the Neverember boy to the boss. The other one is useless -- dispose of him.'

content_hints.description: A narrow passage is hidden behind a false wall of crates. The wooden planks here are scuffed and scratched from repeated use, sloping downward into a damp cellar beneath the warehouse, which is the Zhentarim's actual base of operations.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a narrow passage descend into darkness beyond the false wall. The planks underfoot are scuffed and scratched from heavy use, and the air grows colder and damper with every step. The walls press close, barely wide enough for two abreast, and the passage slopes steadily downward into a cellar the warehouse above was built to hide.
content_hints.perception_success: One stack of crates is mounted on a wooden rail. It slides aside to reveal a passage descending into darkness.

content_hints.description: The front of this bookstore is adorned with a charming sign of a gold dragon curled around a treasure hoard of books and scrolls. Inside, the shop is decorated with beautiful hardwood, and the earthy scent of old books permeates the air. The library fills two floors of this three-story building.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see piles of paper, twisted pieces of metal, and bird droppings littering the space. Rocking chairs and a trellis table sit amidst the charred remains of a bookshelf, all shrouded in thick cobwebs. Arrow slits line the walls, and pigeons nest in the rafters above.

npcs.name: Floon Blagmaar
npcs.dialogue: Thank the gods! Please -- get me out of here! They took Renaer somewhere else. The big one -- Krentz -- kept asking about gold. Some kind of vault. I don't know anything about a vault, I swear!
npcs.notes: Volo's friend. Was mistaken for Renaer Neverember due to similar appearance. Knows nothing of value but believes Renaer is in greater danger.

loot.name: Floon's Signet Ring
loot.description: A gold ring bearing the Blagmaar family crest. Floon insists the party keep it as payment.

content_hints.description: The Zhentarim Hideout is a ramshackle two-story warehouse on Castle Lane, standing at the back of an outer yard behind a high fence with an unlocked gate. Inside, the main cellar is a low-ceilinged room supported by timber pillars, lit by guttering torches in iron sconces, with crates of weapons and contraband lining the walls.
content_hints.read_aloud: > You see a ramshackle two-story warehouse on Castle Lane, its gate on the back of a high fence unlocked. Inside, the air is thick with the smell of torch smoke and sweat, and you hear muffled voices repeating phrases like, "Xanathar sends its regards."

enemies.name: Krentz
enemies.notes: Studded leather, longsword, dagger. Former soldier. Will not surrender -- too afraid of his Zhentarim superiors.
special: Multiattack: Two longsword attacks per turn.
Parry: +2 AC as a reaction against one melee attack.

enemies.name: Zhentarim Thug
enemies.notes: Maces, leather armor. Will flee if Krentz falls.

loot.name: Krentz's Longsword
loot.description: Well-maintained but unremarkable. The pommel is shaped like a serpent -- Zhentarim standard issue.

loot.name: Zhentarim Orders
loot.description: A sealed letter: 'Interrogate the Neverember boy. Find the Stone of Golorr. Report to Urstul Floxin at the appointed hour. --M'

npcs.name: Zhentarim
npcs.role: They repeat the following phrases: “Xanathar sends its regards.”
